heading: State flexibility in benefit packages
label: 1396u–7 State flexibility in benefit packages
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1937 of act , was renumbered section 1939 and is classified to .Aug. 14, 1935section 1396v of this title

children.heading: State option of providing benchmark benefits
children.label: a State option of providing benchmark benefits
i
children.text: provides benchmark coverage described in subsection (b)(1) or benchmark equivalent coverage described in subsection (b)(2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: for any individual described in who is eligible under the State plan in accordance with paragraphs (10) and (17) of , consists of the items and services described in (relating to early and periodic screening, diagnostic, and treatment services defined in ) and provided in accordance with the requirements of .section 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396a(a) of this titlesection 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396d(r) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(43) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The State may only exercise the option under subparagraph (A) for an individual eligible under subclause (VIII) of or under an eligibility category that had been established under the State plan on or before .section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(i) of this titleFebruary 8, 2006

children.heading: Option of additional benefits
children.label: C Option of additional benefits
children.text: In the case of coverage described in subparagraph (A), a State, at its option, may provide such additional benefits as the State may specify.

children.heading: Treatment as medical assistance
children.label: D Treatment as medical assistance
children.text: Payment of premiums for such coverage under this subsection shall be treated as payment of other insurance premiums described in the third sentence of .section 1396d(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: requiring a State to offer all or any of the items and services required by subparagraph (A)(ii) through an issuer of benchmark coverage described in subsection (b)(1) or benchmark equivalent coverage described in subsection (b)(2);

children.label: ii
children.text: preventing a State from offering all or any of the items and services required by subparagraph (A)(ii) through an issuer of benchmark coverage described in subsection (b)(1) or benchmark equivalent coverage described in subsection (b)(2); or

children.label: iii
children.text: affecting a child’s entitlement to care and services described in subsections (a)(4)(B) and (r) of and provided in accordance with whether provided through benchmark coverage, benchmark equivalent coverage, or otherwise.section 1396d of this titlesection 1396a(a)(43) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: ensures necessary transportation for individuals enrolled under such package or coverage to and from providers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides a description of the methods that will be used to ensure such transportation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), a State may require that a full-benefit eligible individual (as defined in subparagraph (C)) within a group obtain benefits under this subchapter through enrollment in coverage described in paragraph (1)(A). A State may apply the previous sentence to individuals within 1 or more groups of such individuals.

children.heading: Mandatory pregnant women
children.label: i Mandatory pregnant women
children.text: The individual is a pregnant woman who is required to be covered under the State plan under .section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(i) of this title

children.heading: Blind or disabled individuals
children.label: ii Blind or disabled individuals
children.text: The individual qualifies for medical assistance under the State plan on the basis of being blind or disabled (or being treated as being blind or disabled) without regard to whether the individual is eligible for supplemental security income benefits under subchapter XVI on the basis of being blind or disabled and including an individual who is eligible for medical assistance on the basis of .section 1396a(e)(3) of this title

children.heading: Dual eligibles
children.label: iii Dual eligibles
children.text: The individual is entitled to benefits under any part of subchapter XVIII.

children.heading: Terminally ill hospice patients
children.label: iv Terminally ill hospice patients
children.text: The individual is terminally ill and is receiving benefits for hospice care under this subchapter.

children.heading: Eligible on basis of institutionalization
children.label: v Eligible on basis of institutionalization
children.text: The individual is an inpatient in a hospital, nursing facility, intermediate care facility for the mentally retarded, or other medical institution, and is required, as a condition of receiving services in such institution under the State plan, to spend for costs of medical care all but a minimal amount of the individual’s income required for personal needs.

children.heading: Medically frail and special medical needs individuals
children.label: vi Medically frail and special medical needs individuals
children.text: The individual is medically frail or otherwise an individual with special medical needs (as identified in accordance with regulations of the Secretary).

children.heading: Beneficiaries qualifying for long-term care services
children.label: vii Beneficiaries qualifying for long-term care services
children.text: The individual qualifies based on medical condition for medical assistance for long-term care services described in .section 1396p(c)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Children in foster care receiving child welfare services and children receiving foster care or adoption assistance
children.label: viii Children in foster care receiving child welfare services and children receiving foster care or adoption assistance
children.text: The individual is an individual with respect to whom child welfare services are made available under part B of subchapter IV on the basis of being a child in foster care or with respect to whom adoption or foster care assistance is made available under part E of such subchapter, without regard to age, or the individual qualifies for medical assistance on the basis of .section 1396a(a)(10)(A)(i)(IX) of this title

children.heading: TANF and section 1396u–1 parents
children.label: ix TANF and section 1396u–1 parents
children.text: The individual qualifies for medical assistance on the basis of eligibility to receive assistance under a State plan funded under part A of subchapter IV (as in effect on or after the welfare reform effective date defined in ).section 1396u–1(i) of this title

children.heading: Women in the breast or cervical cancer program
children.label: x Women in the breast or cervical cancer program
children.text: The individual is a woman who is receiving medical assistance by virtue of the application of sections 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XVIII) and 1396a(aa) of this title.

children.label: I
children.text: qualifies for medical assistance on the basis of ; orsection 1396a(a)(10)(A)(ii)(XII) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: is not a qualified alien (as defined in ) and receives care and services necessary for the treatment of an emergency medical condition in accordance with .section 1641 of title 8section 1396b(v) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, subject to clause (ii), the term “full-benefit eligible individual” means for a State for a month an individual who is determined eligible by the State for medical assistance for all services defined in which are covered under the State plan under this subchapter for such month under or under any other category of eligibility for medical assistance for all such services under this subchapter, as determined by the Secretary.section 1396d(a) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(10)(A) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion of medically needy and spend-down populations
children.label: ii Exclusion of medically needy and spend-down populations
children.text: Such term shall not include an individual determined to be eligible by the State for medical assistance under or by reason of or otherwise eligible based on a reduction of income based on costs incurred for medical or other remedial care.section 1396a(a)(10)(C) of this titlesection 1396a(f) of this title

children.heading: FEHBP-equivalent health insurance coverage
children.label: A FEHBP-equivalent health insurance coverage
children.text: The standard Blue Cross/Blue Shield preferred provider option service benefit plan, described in and offered under .section 8903(1) of title 5

children.heading: State employee coverage
children.label: B State employee coverage
children.text: A health benefits coverage plan that is offered and generally available to State employees in the State involved.

children.label: i
children.text: is offered by a health maintenance organization (as defined in ), andsection 300gg–91(b)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: has the largest insured commercial, non-medicaid enrollment of covered lives of such coverage plans offered by such a health maintenance organization in the State involved.

children.heading: Secretary-approved coverage
children.label: D Secretary-approved coverage
children.text: Any other health benefits coverage that the Secretary determines, upon application by a State, provides appropriate coverage for the population proposed to be provided such coverage.

children.label: i
children.text: Inpatient and outpatient hospital services.

children.label: ii
children.text: Physicians’ surgical and medical services.

children.label: iii
children.text: Laboratory and x-ray services.

children.label: iv
children.text: Coverage of prescription drugs.

children.label: v
children.text: Mental health services.

children.label: vi
children.text: Well-baby and well-child care, including age-appropriate immunizations.

children.label: vii
children.text: Other appropriate preventive services, as designated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Aggregate actuarial value equivalent to benchmark package
children.label: B Aggregate actuarial value equivalent to benchmark package
children.text: The coverage has an aggregate actuarial value that is at least actuarially equivalent to one of the benchmark benefit packages described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Substantial actuarial value for additional services included in benchmark package
children.label: C Substantial actuarial value for additional services included in benchmark package
i
children.text: Vision services.

children.label: ii
children.text: Hearing services.

children.label: A
children.text: by an individual who is a member of the American Academy of Actuaries;

children.label: B
children.text: using generally accepted actuarial principles and methodologies;

children.label: C
children.text: using a standardized set of utilization and price factors;

children.label: D
children.text: using a standardized population that is representative of the population involved;

children.label: E
children.text: applying the same principles and factors in comparing the value of different coverage (or categories of services);

children.label: F
children.text: without taking into account any differences in coverage based on the method of delivery or means of cost control or utilization used; and

children.label: G
children.text: taking into account the ability of a State to reduce benefits by taking into account the increase in actuarial value of benefits coverage offered under this subchapter that results from the limitations on cost sharing under such coverage.

children.heading: Coverage of rural health clinic and FQHC services
children.label: 4 Coverage of rural health clinic and FQHC services
A
children.text: the individual has access, through such coverage or otherwise, to services described in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of ; andsection 1396d(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: payment for such services is made in accordance with the requirements of .section 1396a(bb) of this title

children.heading: Minimum standards
children.label: 5 Minimum standards
children.text: Effective , any benchmark benefit package under paragraph (1) or benchmark equivalent coverage under paragraph (2) must provide at least essential health benefits as described in , and beginning , coverage of routine patient costs for items and services furnished in connection with participation in a qualifying clinical trial (as defined in ).January 1, 2014January 1, 2022section 18022(b) of this titlesection 1396d(gg) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any benchmark benefit package under paragraph (1) or benchmark equivalent coverage under paragraph (2) that is offered by an entity that is not a medicaid managed care organization and that provides both medical and surgical benefits and mental health or substance use disorder benefits, the entity shall ensure that the financial requirements and treatment limitations applicable to such mental health or substance use disorder benefits comply with the requirements of in the same manner as such requirements apply to a group health plan. In applying the previous sentence with respect to requirements under paragraph (8) of , a benchmark benefit package or benchmark equivalent coverage described in such sentence shall be treated as in compliance with such requirements if the State plan under this subchapter or the benchmark benefit package or benefit equivalent coverage, as applicable, is in compliance with subpart C of part 440 of title 42, Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor regulation.section 300gg–26(a) of this titlesection 300gg–26(a) of this title

children.heading: Deemed compliance
children.label: B Deemed compliance
children.text: Coverage provided with respect to an individual described in and covered under the State plan under of the services described in (relating to early and periodic screening, diagnostic, and treatment services defined in ) and provided in accordance with , shall be deemed to satisfy the requirements of subparagraph (A).section 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(10)(A) of this titlesection 1396d(a)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1396d(r) of this titlesection 1396a(a)(43) of this title

children.heading: Coverage of family planning services and supplies
children.label: 7 Coverage of family planning services and supplies
children.text: Notwithstanding the previous provisions of this section, a State may not provide for medical assistance through enrollment of an individual with benchmark coverage or benchmark-equivalent coverage under this section unless such coverage includes for any individual described in , medical assistance for family planning services and supplies in accordance with such section.section 1396d(a)(4)(C) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: COVID–19 vaccines and administration of the vaccines; and

children.label: B
children.text: testing and treatments for COVID–19, including specialized equipment and therapies (including preventive therapies), and, in the case of such an individual who is diagnosed with or presumed to have COVID–19, during the period such individual has (or is presumed to have) COVID–19, the treatment of a condition that may seriously complicate the treatment of COVID–19, if otherwise covered under the State plan (or waiver of such plan).

children.heading: Publication of provisions affected
children.label: c Publication of provisions affected
children.text: With respect to a State plan amendment to provide benchmark benefits in accordance with subsections (a) and (b) that is approved by the Secretary, the Secretary shall publish on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, a list of the provisions of this subchapter that the Secretary has determined do not apply in order to enable the State to carry out the plan amendment and the reason for each such determination on the date such approval is made, and shall publish such list in the Federal Register and not later than 30 days after such date of approval.2So in original.2
